contributingFactors: CPU request of 50m per pod is very low, causing HPA to perceive utilization as 198% even when pods are only using 99–100m
CPU limit of 100m per pod is tight, causing all pods to be throttled at the ceiling
HPA maxReplicas is set to 3, preventing scale-out beyond the current replica count
HPA has been at max replicas since 2026-05-03T20:05:30Z (ScalingLimited condition: TooManyReplicas)
Desired replica count would be ~12 based on current utilization ratio (198%/50% × 3), far beyond the configured max
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas because all pods are CPU-throttled at their 100m limit (198% utilization vs 50% target), and the HPA maxReplicas is set too low to absorb the load. The HPA controller computed ~12 replicas are needed but is blocked by maxReplicas: 3. Contributing factors include undersized CPU requests (50m) amplifying apparent utilization, and a hard CPU limit (100m) causing throttling.

analysis.rootCauseSummary: HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas because all pods are CPU-throttled at their 100m limit (198% utilization vs 50% target), and the HPA maxReplicas is set too low to absorb the load. The HPA controller computed ~12 replicas are needed but is blocked by maxReplicas: 3. Contributing factors include undersized CPU requests (50m) amplifying apparent utilization, and a hard CPU limit (100m) causing throttling.

selectedWorkflow.rationale: patch-hpa-v1 directly addresses the root cause: the HPA maxReplicas ceiling of 3 is too low for the current workload demand. The workflow patches the HPA to raise maxReplicas, unblocking the HPA controller from scaling to the ~12 replicas it has computed as necessary. The workflow's when-to-use criteria match exactly — HPA at maxReplicas with legitimate traffic pressure (198% CPU utilization vs 50% target). The when-not-to-use condition (bug/runaway process) does not apply; the load is consistent and real. NEW_MAX_REPLICAS is set to 12 to match the HPA controller's own desired replica calculation.

kaResponse.analysis: HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas because all pods are CPU-throttled at their 100m limit (198% utilization vs 50% target), and the HPA maxReplicas is set too low to absorb the load. The HPA controller computed ~12 replicas are needed but is blocked by maxReplicas: 3. Contributing factors include undersized CPU requests (50m) amplifying apparent utilization, and a hard CPU limit (100m) causing throttling.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas because all pods are CPU-throttled at their 100m limit (198% utilization vs 50% target), and the HPA maxReplicas is set too low to absorb the load. The HPA controller computed ~12 replicas are needed but is blocked by maxReplicas: 3. Contributing factors include undersized CPU requests (50m) amplifying apparent utilization, and a hard CPU limit (100m) causing throttling.
causalChain: Signal: KubeHpaMaxedOut — HPA api-frontend has been at maxReplicas=3 continuously since 20:05:30Z
Why? HPA desiredReplicas exceeds maxReplicas: current CPU utilization is 198% vs 50% target, requiring ~12 replicas to satisfy the target
Why? All 3 pods are consuming 99–100m CPU, hitting their hard CPU limit of 100m and being throttled — actual workload demand exceeds per-pod capacity
Why? CPU request is set to only 50m per pod, making 99m usage appear as 198% utilization to the HPA scaling algorithm

Root cause: HPA maxReplicas=3 is too low for the actual traffic load, and the Deployment's CPU request (50m) is undersized relative to real usage (~99m), causing the HPA to be permanently scale-limited and unable to distribute load sufficiently

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The primary remediation target is the HPA (increase maxReplicas to allow scale-out). The Deployment CPU request/limit adjustment is a contributing fix. The HPA is the most directly actionable resource — increasing maxReplicas immediately unblocks scaling. The Deployment is listed as a contributing factor.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The fix is targeted: increase HPA maxReplicas and optionally adjust Deployment CPU requests. This directly addresses the scaling bottleneck without affecting other workloads. The HPA is the single resource blocking relief.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: HPA spec and status, HPA events, Deployment spec and status, all 3 pods (status, resource config), live CPU usage via kubectl top, Prometheus (unauthorized). Did not check: node capacity details, cluster autoscaler (not relevant — pods are Running, not Pending). All resources in the signal chain were examined.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from KubeHpaMaxedOut → at maxReplicas → CPU utilization 198% → pods throttled at 100m limit → CPU request 50m undersized → maxReplicas too low. Cannot go deeper without application-level traffic analysis, but the Kubernetes configuration root cause is fully identified.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All key claims are backed by tool output: HPA status shows currentReplicas=3, desiredReplicas=3, averageUtilization=198% (kubectl_describe + kubectl_get_by_name). ScalingLimited condition confirmed with reason TooManyReplicas (HPA status). Pod CPU usage confirmed at 99–100m against 100m limit via kubectl_top_pods. HPA events confirm scale-out to 3 at 20:05:30Z. Prometheus was unauthorized — no historical trend data available.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history for HPA api-frontend or Deployment api-frontend in demo-hpa namespace (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context for both resources). N/A — no prior failed remediations to avoid.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Traffic spike causing genuine demand surge — supported by evidence (pods are CPU-saturated). (2) Misconfigured CPU requests causing inflated utilization ratio — also confirmed (50m request vs 99m usage). (3) Node resource pressure preventing scheduling of more pods — ruled out: 3 pods are Running and healthy across 3 different nodes with no node pressure events observed. (4) HPA metric collection failure — early events show FailedGetResourceMetric, but later events confirm successful metric collection and scaling.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0: -0.05 for Prometheus being unauthorized (no historical CPU trend to confirm duration/pattern of saturation), -0.03 for not verifying node-level capacity (could there be a scheduling bottleneck preventing more pods if maxReplicas were increased — not verified). Final confidence: 0.92.

contributingFactors: CPU request of 50m per pod is very low, causing HPA to perceive utilization as 198% even when pods are only using 99–100m
CPU limit of 100m per pod is tight, causing all pods to be throttled at the ceiling
HPA maxReplicas is set to 3, preventing scale-out beyond the current replica count
HPA has been at max replicas since 2026-05-03T20:05:30Z (ScalingLimited condition: TooManyReplicas)
Desired replica count would be ~12 based on current utilization ratio (198%/50% × 3), far beyond the configured max
